title: crucianella exasperata
parts: Crucianella exasperata has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, which contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, and are used to treat various inflammatory conditions. The roots of this plant contain alkaloids and terpenoids, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and as an expectorant. The stems and flowers of Crucianella exasperata are rich in essential oils, including limonene and pinene, which have been used to treat digestive problems and as an antiseptic. Additionally, the plant's underground stems, or rhizomes, contain saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions and as an antifungal agent.
